text: Neuroscience has always been a prime source of inspiration for work in AI. Now we believe it is time for a new field of fundamental neuroscience research for the development of NeuroAI. An essential aspect of this effort is what we call the “embodied Turing test” and an AI system that is able to successfully pass it. Instead of basing tests on capabilities unique to humans such as playing chess or speaking languages, in the embodied Turing test an AI system needs to interact effectively in the physical world much like a small animal and with the sensorimotor skills animals of all kinds have evolved over half a billion years of animal evolution. Thus instead of aiming for exceptionally human intelligence in an AI system as is the case with today’s weak AI and strong AI, the aim would be to reach a lower level of very general animal-like intelligence which can be sustained in embodied computational vehicles of a different design. The AI systems that could pass the embodied Turing test would be on a par with the basic animal ability to survive, and their design would serve as a guide for how to develop efficient, versatile and intelligent robots.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
